candidate_problems.text: What are the minimal structural conditions and threshold dynamics that cause a system to tip from a high-trust equilibrium into a distrust lock-in, and are there reliable early-warning indicators of that transition?

How can trust as 'coordination infrastructure' be operationalized and measured separately from its substitutes (audits, contracts, surveillance, platforms), especially when those substitutes can both compensate for and further erode trust?

Under what conditions do trust-repair interventions actually shift payoffs back toward stable high-trust equilibria rather than merely adding more compliance machinery, and what sequence of changes creates durable escape from distrust traps?
